analyst_briefing: CRISIS regime VERY HIGH conviction confirmed: system_average -0.686 with all 8 asset classes BEARISH/MILD NEGATIVE, stable from Apr 28 DR-Computer baseline. SA structural signals dominate: Hormuz closure (5-10 ships/day, ECB/IEA largest disruption history), Liberation Day tariffs T3 (11.8% effective, ECB '1930s sweeping'), US fiscal $9.8T wall/$1T interest. No regime change (<3 corroborators test passed); TS noise in fx_swap_basis ELEVATED (partial ceasefire normalize) and dollar_weaponization WARNING (COT squeeze risk) ignored for call stability. Asset scores at baseline (deviation=0), directions correct; blind spots active in Crypto/Tech/Consumer Staples (M2 mirage, earnings suppression). Tail risks: Hormuz re-closure (0.45x0.9, ERM), tariff expiry/pharma spike (0.6x0.8 non-consensus), Treasury tsunami (0.4x0.85). Cross-flags: ERM HIGH (oil/jet rationing), ESA HIGH (ECB revisions/spread), SCEM MEDIUM (fiscal/weaponization). No US/non-US divergence; global alignment on dual shocks. Forward: monitor Hormuz traffic, Q2 guidance, 30yr auctions for confirmation/break.
